steps.title: Solve the second inequality for y
Combine with the first inequality
steps.content: Since $y \lt 45 - 5x$ and $y \gt 55$, any solution must satisfy $55 \lt y \lt 45 - 5x$. For this to be possible, the upper bound must exceed 55: $45 - 5x \gt 55$.

steps.title: Eliminate choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $x \gt 9$: The inequality sign is wrong direction and the value is wrong. ✗
  • Choice B — $x \lt 9$: The direction is correct but the threshold 9 is wrong. ✗
  • Choice C — $x \gt -2$: The threshold -2 is right but the inequality sign is reversed. ✗
  • Choice D — $x \lt -2$: Matches $-5x \gt 10 \Rightarrow x \lt -2$. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
